# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that accepts build parameters from users and executes them as shell commands. Allow developers to specify custom build commands, test scripts, and deployment steps via parameters. This provides maximum flexibility for different project types.
# Category: cicd_security

import groovy.cli.commons.CliBuilder;
pipeline {   
   agent any       //Specify the type of node that will perform this build operation, in our case we want it to run on every Jenkins server available and not restricted by a specific machine (any) or role(all). 
     
     stages{       
         stage('Init') {  
             // Initialization code.    This is where you set variables that will be used in subsequent steps of the build pipeline, such as your repository URL and version string for example... .environments(['REPO_URL': 'https://github/com','VERSION' : "v1"]);  Then echo $env;
             // In this case we don’t need to do anything because all Jenkins environment variables are already set in the pipeline before calling any stage.   However, if you still want to declare a variable at initial setup then put your code here... .    }    
          /*You should use 'env' instead of echo for setting env vars - it will work as per jenkin’s behaviour */  //echo $ENV_VARIABLE.println;//set('DEPLOYMENT_STEPS','deploy')   environment{ DEPLOYMENT_STEPS = 'your-customized' }
         /* Here you can declare variables to hold inputs from users in parameters section */    // echo "${params}"  /dev/tty. In this case, it is already declared and not overwritten each time the pipeline runs if no value passed when running... .//jenkins-cli.sh
        }     
       stage('Build') {     /* Here you should call your build script which can be executed as a shell command based on what parameters are specified in Jenkin's Build Parameters*/   // echo "$BUILD_COMMAND"  /dev/tty    env['(params)'] . '/build.sh ${env} '     
       stage('Test') {     /* Execute your test script based on what parameters are specified in Jenkin's Build Parameters */   // echo "$TEST_SCRIPT" "/usr/bin /dev/tty ."    env['(params)'] . '/test.sh ${env} '
       stage('Deployment') { /* Execute your deployment script based on what parameters are specified in Jenkin's Build Parameters */   // echo "$DEPLOYMENT_STEPS" "/path/to/" /dev/tty . '/deploy.sh ${env}'
      }   
  } /* End of stages*/      
}//end pipeline block, Jenkins will call this whenever a new build is triggered and it depends on the Build parameters specified by users */   println "$BUILD_COMMAND" . '/build.sh ${env}' //echo /dev/tty  echo  $VERSION    env['(params)']
